title: An AI just broke an 87-year-old maths problem, in a tweet sent during the World Cup final
description: On Sunday evening, as Spain and Argentina played out the World Cup final, the mathematician Levent Alpöge posted a short message on X. The Jacobian conjecture, he wrote, is false. He thanked a friend for asking about it, and another friend, “fable,” for worki…

title: Human mathematicians are being outcounterexampled
description: It’s been an interesting few weeks for counterexamples. This post is basically my perspective of what has been going on in the world of formalization, AI tools and, in particular, counterexam…

title: Bipartite Perfect Matching in Deterministic NC
description: Nutan Limaye and Thore Husfeldt guest post on the new deterministic parallel algorithm for bipartite perfect matching  by Abhranil Chatterje...

title: Quote of the Day by Bernhard Riemann: "If only I had the theorems..." — Inspiring life lessons from the renowned German mathematician on curiosity, discovery, innovation, and why great ideas come before perfect answers
description: Quote of the Day by Bernhard Riemann: "If only I had the theorems! Then I should find the proofs easily enough." One remarkable thought from the renowned German mathematician still challenges the modern world. Why do the greatest discoveries begin with an ide…

title: ChatGPT just proved another 50-year-old math conjecture
description: What’s the secret to prompting an AI to solve math problems that have left humans stumped? Tell it to believe in itself

title: Computation as a Universal and Fundamental Concept
description: TIM ROUGHGARDEN treats computation as a law of nature, tracing its arc from Turing's proof that some problems are unsolvable to the P versus NP question at the heart of modern computer science.

title: Dropping in on Gottfried Leibniz (2013)
description: Visit to archive provides a look at Leibniz’s papers, notation, calculator. His near misses on universal computation, quest for the systemization & formalization of knowledge.

title: A digestion of unit distance constructions

title: The Fall of the Theorem Economy
description: How AI could destroy mathematics and barely touch it

title: Appointment of Regius Professor of Mathematics in the University of Oxford: 1 July 2026
description: The King has approved that Professor James Maynard FRS, Professor of Number Theory at the Mathematical Institute, University of Oxford, be appointed Regius Professor of Mathematics in the University of Oxford in succession to Professor Sir Andrew Wiles.

title: The Riemann Hypothesis manifested in dynamical quantum phase transitions
description: The authors establish a formal correspondence between the Riemann Hypothesis and engineered quantum many-body dynamics. By constructing a Riemann-based Hamiltonian, they demonstrate that the hypothesis manifests through quantum phase transitions.
